UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the DocuExtract application. This entity stores basic user information linked to documents and extractions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and linking with external authentication systems.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded image or document file by a user for text extraction and analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this document. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document)

originalFileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

originalFileUrl.description: A URL or path indicating where the original document file is stored.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded document (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

processingStatus.description: Current processing status of the document (e.g., 'uploaded', 'processing', 'processed', 'failed').

documentType.description: Categorization of the document content, guiding specific AI extraction (e.g., 'identity_proof', 'billing', 'medical_report', 'general', 'other').

extractedRawText.description: The full, unedited text extracted from the document by the OCR/AI system.

lastEditedAt.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the extracted raw text by a user.

IdentityProofExtraction.title: IdentityProofExtraction
IdentityProofExtraction.description: Stores structured data extracted from identity proof documents such as passports, driver's licenses, or national IDs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IdentityProofExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the original Document from which this data was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 IdentityProofExtraction)

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the identity proof details were extracted.

fullName.description: The full name of the person as extracted from the identity document.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the person.

address.description: The residential address extracted from the document.

documentNumber.description: The unique identification number of the document (e.g., passport number, driver's license number).

issueDate.description: The date when the document was issued.

expiryDate.description: The date when the document expires.

nationality.description: The nationality of the document holder.

countryOfIssue.description: The country that issued the document.

BillingExtraction.title: BillingExtraction
BillingExtraction.description: Stores structured data extracted from billing documents like invoices, receipts, or utility bills.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillingExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the original Document from which this data was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 BillingExtraction)

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the billing details were extracted.

vendorName.description: The name of the vendor or service provider.

vendorAddress.description: The address of the vendor or service provider.

invoiceDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the payment is due.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the bill, including taxes and fees.

currency.description: The currency of the total amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

subtotal.description: The subtotal amount before taxes or discounts.

taxAmount.description: The total amount of tax applied.

lineItems.description: An array of individual items or services listed on the bill.

MedicalReportExtraction.title: MedicalReportExtraction
MedicalReportExtraction.description: Stores structured data extracted from medical or hospital reports, including diagnoses and findings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalReportExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the original Document from which this data was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 MedicalReportExtraction)

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the medical report details were extracted.

patientName.description: The name of the patient as recorded in the report.

reportDate.description: The date when the medical report was issued.

hospitalName.description: The name of the hospital or clinic where the report was generated.

physicianName.description: The name of the attending physician or doctor.

diagnosis.description: An array of diagnosed conditions or diseases.

findings.description: A summary or detailed description of medical findings.

recommendedTreatment.description: Details about the recommended treatment plan.

labResultsSummary.description: A summary of relevant laboratory test results.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is strictly restricted to the owning user. The document ID `{userId}` for each UserProfile document must match the `UserProfile.id` field and `request.auth.uid` for secure access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores documents uploaded by a user. Access is restricted to the owning user. The document ID `{documentId}` matches the `Document.id` field, and the `Document.userId` field must match `{userId}` for QAP compliance. This hierarchical structure enables simple and secure `list` operations for a user's documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded document.

definition.description: Stores structured identity proof data extracted from a document. Access is restricted to the owning user of the parent document. This collection includes a denormalized 'userId' field (copied from the parent Document) for authorization independence, allowing atomic security checks without requiring 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document from which this data was extracted.

params.name: extractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific identity proof extraction.

definition.description: Stores structured billing data extracted from a document. Access is restricted to the owning user of the parent document. This collection includes a denormalized 'userId' field (copied from the parent Document) for authorization independence, allowing atomic security checks without requiring 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document from which this data was extracted.

params.name: extractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific billing extraction.

definition.description: Stores structured medical report data extracted from a document. Access is restricted to the owning user of the parent document. This collection includes a denormalized 'userId' field (copied from the parent Document) for authorization independence, allowing atomic security checks without requiring 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document from which this data was extracted.

params.name: extractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific medical report extraction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for DocuExtract is designed with a strong emphasis on user-centric data ownership, robust security, and simplified rule management. All user-specific data, including profiles, uploaded documents, and their corresponding AI-extracted details, are nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This pattern ensures that all data related to a specific user is logically grouped and secured.

**Authorization Independence:** This design critically implements Authorization Independence through strategic denormalization. Each `Document` document includes a `userId` field that matches the parent collection's `{userId}` wildcard. More importantly, every subcollection document, such as `IdentityProofExtraction`, `BillingExtraction`, and `MedicalReportExtraction`, will also explicitly include the `userId` of the owning user, denormalized from its parent `Document`. This means that security rules for any `*Extraction` document can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without performing expensive and non-atomic `get()` operations on parent documents. This prevents security rule complexity, avoids transaction issues, and enhances debuggability.

**Querying for All Permissions (QAPs):** The hierarchical structure directly supports secure `list` operations, adhering to the QAPs principle (rules are not filters). By placing documents under `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}`, any query for documents implicitly requires the `userId` to be known. Firebase Security Rules can then enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches `{userId}` in the path, ensuring users can only list and access their own documents and their associated extractions. This inherently limits the scope of queries to the authenticated user's data, preventing accidental exposure or unauthorized listing of other users' information.

**Structural Segregation:** Different types of extracted data (Identity Proof, Billing, Medical Report) are segregated into distinct subcollections (e.g., `/identityProofExtractions`, `/billingExtractions`). While all belong to a parent `Document`, this segregation ensures each subcollection maintains a homogeneous security posture and schema, simplifying rule writing and data management. It also adheres to the principle of not mixing data with different access needs within the same collection. The `Document.documentType` field allows for categorization at the parent level, guiding which specific extraction subcollection might be populated.
